action_ref.content:
{
  "analysis": "Great! R installation completed successfully. Now we have R and Rscript available. Next step is to install glmnet package. We'll run install.packages with repos and also ensure dependencies. Then we can write the R script.",
  "plan": "Install glmnet, then create the R script. We'll also install maybe 'qpcR' for AICc but we can compute manually. We'll write a script that loads data, splits, fits models, evaluates, and writes outputs. Let's proceed.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "Rscript -e 'install.packages("glmnet", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")' 2>&1 | tail -10
",
      "duration": 30.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "Rscript -e 'library(glmnet)'
",
      "duration": 1.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > run.R << 'EOF'
# Load required library
library(glmnet)

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(42)

# Load the data
load("fMRIdata.RData")

# Dimensions
print(dim(fit_feat))
print(dim(resp_dat))
print(dim(val_feat))
print(dim(loc_dat))

# Split training data into 50% train, 25% validation, 25% test
n <- nrow(fit_feat)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = floor(0.5 * n))
remaining <- setdiff(1:n, train_idx)
val_idx <- sample(remaining, size = floor(0.5 * length(remaining)))
test_idx <- setdiff(remaining, val_idx)

X_train <- fit_feat[train_idx, ]
y_train <- resp_dat[train_idx, ]
X_val <- fit_feat[val_idx, ]
y_val <- resp_dat[val_idx, ]
X_test <- fit_feat[test_idx, ]
y_test <- resp_dat[test_idx, ]

# For each voxel, we will fit models and evaluate
# We'll focus on voxel 1 for final prediction
voxel <- 1

# Function to compute AICc from glmnet object (for a given lambda)
# glmnet does not provide log-likelihood directly; we can compute using deviance
# deviance = -2 * log-likelihood up to constant? Actually deviance is -2 * log-likelihood + constant.
# We'll use deviance from glmnet (dev.ratio) to compute log-likelihood.
# Reference: https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/138569
# We'll compute AIC and AICc for each lambda using deviance and degrees of freedom.
compute_aic <- function(fit, X, y, lambda_idx) {
  n <- nrow(X)
  # deviance explained: 1 - dev.ratio = deviance/null deviance
  # total deviance: dev.ratio * nulldev
  # We need log-likelihood: logL = -0.5 * deviance + constant (ignored)
  # Since we compare models with same data, constant cancels.
  # We'll compute deviance from glmnet's dev.ratio
  # First get nulldev: deviance of intercept-only model
  # glmnet stores nulldev in fit$nulldev
  nulldev <- fit$nulldev
  dev <- (1 - fit$dev.ratio[lambda_idx]) * nulldev
  k <- fit$df[lambda_idx] + 1  # include intercept
  aic <- dev + 2 * k
  aicc <- aic + (2 * k * (k + 1)) / (n - k - 1)
  return(list(aic = aic, aicc = aicc))
}

# LASSO with CV
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
lasso_cv_lambda <- lasso_cv$lambda.min
lasso_cv_pred <- predict(lasso_cv, newx = X_test, s = lasso_cv_lambda)
lasso_cv_cor <- cor(lasso_cv_pred, y_test[, voxel])
lasso_cv_mse <- mean((lasso_cv_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
lasso_cv_df <- predict(lasso_cv, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_cv_lambda)@i[-1]  # exclude intercept
lasso_cv_df_length <- length(lasso_cv_df)

# LASSO with AIC and AICc selection on validation set
# Fit glmnet on training set with many lambdas
lasso_full <- glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 1)
# Predict on validation set for each lambda
pred_val <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_val)
# Compute deviance on validation set? Actually AIC/AICc should be computed on training set (log-likelihood) but penalized.
# We'll compute AIC/AICc using training data as per definition.
# We'll compute for each lambda and choose lambda minimizing AIC or AICc.
n_train <- nrow(X_train)
aic_vals <- numeric(length(lasso_full$lambda))
aicc_vals <- numeric(length(lasso_full$lambda))
for (i in seq_along(lasso_full$lambda)) {
  aic_info <- compute_aic(lasso_full, X_train, y_train[, voxel], i)
  aic_vals[i] <- aic_info$aic
  aicc_vals[i] <- aic_info$aicc
}
idx_aic <- which.min(aic_vals)
idx_aicc <- which.min(aicc_vals)
lasso_aic_lambda <- lasso_full$lambda[idx_aic]
lasso_aicc_lambda <- lasso_full$lambda[idx_aicc]
# Evaluate on test set
lasso_aic_pred <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_test, s = lasso_aic_lambda)
lasso_aic_cor <- cor(lasso_aic_pred, y_test[, voxel])
lasso_aic_mse <- mean((lasso_aic_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
lasso_aic_df <- predict(lasso_full, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_aic_lambda)@i[-1]
lasso_aic_df_length <- length(lasso_aic_df)

lasso_aicc_pred <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_test, s = lasso_aicc_lambda)
lasso_aicc_cor <- cor(lasso_aicc_pred, y_test[, voxel])
lasso_aicc_mse <- mean((lasso_aicc_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
lasso_aicc_df <- predict(lasso_full, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_aicc_lambda)@i[-1]
lasso_aicc_df_length <- length(lasso_aicc_df)

# Ridge regression with CV
ridge_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
ridge_cv_lambda <- ridge_cv$lambda.min
ridge_cv_pred <- predict(ridge_cv, newx = X_test, s = ridge_cv_lambda)
ridge_cv_cor <- cor(ridge_cv_pred, y_test[, voxel])
ridge_cv_mse <- mean((ridge_cv_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
ridge_cv_df <- predict(ridge_cv, type = "coefficients", s = ridge_cv_lambda)@i[-1]
ridge_cv_df_length <- length(ridge_cv_df)

# Create model comparison dataframe
model_names <- c("LASSO_CV", "LASSO_AIC", "LASSO_AICc", "Ridge_CV")
cor_vals <- c(lasso_cv_cor, lasso_aic_cor, lasso_aicc_cor, ridge_cv_cor)
mse_vals <- c(lasso_cv_mse, lasso_aic_mse, lasso_aicc_mse, ridge_cv_mse)
lambda_vals <- c(lasso_cv_lambda, lasso_aic_lambda, lasso_aicc_lambda, ridge_cv_lambda)
df_vals <- c(lasso_cv_df_length, lasso_aic_df_length, lasso_aicc_df_length, ridge_cv_df_length)

model_comp <- data.frame(Model = model_names,
                         Correlation = cor_vals,
                         MSE = mse_vals,
                         Lambda = lambda_vals,
                         Df = df_vals)
print(model_comp)

# Write model comparison CSV
write.csv(model_comp, "/app/model_comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Determine best model based on highest correlation (or lowest MSE)
best_idx <- which.max(cor_vals)
best_model_name <- model_names[best_idx]
cat("Best model:", best_model_name, "\n")

# Retrain best model on combined training+validation+test data for final prediction
X_all <- fit_feat  # all 1750 images
y_all <- resp_dat[, voxel]
# For LASSO models, we need to refit with selected lambda
if (best_model_name == "LASSO_CV") {
  best_fit <- cv.glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
  best_lambda <- best_fit$lambda.min
} else if (best_model_name == "LASSO_AIC") {
  # Fit full glmnet to get lambda at AIC minimum on whole data
  full_fit <- glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1)
  # Compute AIC for each lambda
  aic_all <- sapply(seq_along(full_fit$lambda), function(i) {
    compute_aic(full_fit, X_all, y_all, i)$aic
  })
  best_lambda <- full_fit$lambda[which.min(aic_all)]
  best_fit <- full_fit
} else if (best_model_name == "LASSO_AICc") {
  full_fit <- glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1)
  aicc_all <- sapply(seq_along(full_fit$lambda), function(i) {
    compute_aic(full_fit, X_all, y_all, i)$aicc
  })
  best_lambda <- full_fit$lambda[which.min(aicc_all)]
  best_fit <- full_fit
} else { # Ridge_CV
  best_fit <- cv.glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
  best_lambda <- best_fit$lambda.min
}

# Predict on validation images (val_feat)
val_pred <- predict(best_fit, newx = val_feat, s = best_lambda)
# Ensure predictions are within range -100 to 100 (they should be)
val_pred <- pmin(pmax(val_pred, -100), 100)
cat("Predictions range:", range(val_pred), "\n")
cat("Number of predictions:", length(val_pred), "\n")

# Write predictions to file
write.table(val_pred, "/app/predv1.txt", row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE)
EOF
",
      "duration": 1.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}

# Load required library
library(glmnet)

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(42)

# Load the data
load("fMRIdata.RData")

# Dimensions
print(dim(fit_feat))
print(dim(resp_dat))
print(dim(val_feat))
print(dim(loc_dat))

# Split training data into 50% train, 25% validation, 25% test
n <- nrow(fit_feat)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = floor(0.5 * n))
remaining <- setdiff(1:n, train_idx)
val_idx <- sample(remaining, size = floor(0.5 * length(remaining)))
test_idx <- setdiff(remaining, val_idx)

X_train <- fit_feat[train_idx, ]
y_train <- resp_dat[train_idx, ]
X_val <- fit_feat[val_idx, ]
y_val <- resp_dat[val_idx, ]
X_test <- fit_feat[test_idx, ]
y_test <- resp_dat[test_idx, ]

# For each voxel, we will fit models and evaluate
# We'll focus on voxel 1 for final prediction
voxel <- 1

# Function to compute AICc from glmnet object (for a given lambda)
# glmnet does not provide log-likelihood directly; we can compute using deviance
# deviance = -2 * log-likelihood up to constant? Actually deviance is -2 * log-likelihood + constant.
# We'll use deviance from glmnet (dev.ratio) to compute log-likelihood.
# Reference: https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/138569
# We'll compute AIC and AICc for each lambda using deviance and degrees of freedom.
compute_aic <- function(fit, X, y, lambda_idx) {
  n <- nrow(X)
  # deviance explained: 1 - dev.ratio = deviance/null deviance
  # total deviance: dev.ratio * nulldev
  # We need log-likelihood: logL = -0.5 * deviance + constant (ignored)
  # Since we compare models with same data, constant cancels.
  # We'll compute deviance from glmnet's dev.ratio
  # First get nulldev: deviance of intercept-only model
  # glmnet stores nulldev in fit$nulldev
  nulldev <- fit$nulldev
  dev <- (1 - fit$dev.ratio[lambda_idx]) * nulldev
  k <- fit$df[lambda_idx] + 1  # include intercept
  aic <- dev + 2 * k
  aicc <- aic + (2 * k * (k + 1)) / (n - k - 1)
  return(list(aic = aic, aicc = aicc))
}

# LASSO with CV

lasso_cv_df_length <- length(lasso_cv_df)

# Fit glmnet on training set with many lambdas

# Predict on validation set for each lambda
pred_val <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_val)
# Compute deviance on validation set? Actually AIC/AICc should be computed on training set (log-likelihood) but penalized.
# We'll compute AIC/AICc using training data as per definition.
# We'll compute for each lambda and choose lambda minimizing AIC or AICc.
n_train <- nrow(X_train)
aic_vals <- numeric(length(lasso_full$lambda))
aicc_vals <- numeric(length(lasso_full$lambda))
for (i in seq_along(lasso_full$lambda)) {

  aicc_vals[i] <- aic_info$aicc

lasso_aic_mse <- mean((lasso_aic_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)

{
  "analysis": "Great! The terminal is now free. The cat command was interrupted (Ctrl+C) and we are back at the prompt. The glmnet package installed successfully. The script run.R may have been partially created; we need to check if it exists and its content. Let's examine and then either complete it or start fresh with a new script. We'll also need to ensure the script runs correctly and produces the required outputs.",
  "plan": "First, check the content of run.R, see how many lines were written. If it's incomplete, we can overwrite with a new script. We'll write a new script using a simpler approach: create a separate R file with all steps. We'll also need to ensure the AIC/AICc computation is correct; we can use the log-likelihood from glmnet's dev.ratio. We'll also need to compute correlation and MSE for each model. We'll also need to produce model_comparison.csv and predv1.txt. Let's create a new script now.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "head -20 run.R
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "wc -l run.R
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > run.R << 'EOF'
library(glmnet)
set.seed(42)
load("fMRIdata.RData")
# split data
n <- nrow(fit_feat)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = floor(0.5 * n))
remaining <- setdiff(1:n, train_idx)
val_idx <- sample(remaining, size = floor(0.5 * length(remaining)))
test_idx <- setdiff(remaining, val_idx)
X_train <- fit_feat[train_idx, ]
y_train <- resp_dat[train_idx, ]
X_val <- fit_feat[val_idx, ]
y_val <- resp_dat[val_idx, ]
X_test <- fit_feat[test_idx, ]
y_test <- resp_dat[test_idx, ]
voxel <- 1

# function to compute AIC and AICc from glmnet object
compute_aic <- function(fit, X, y, lambda_idx) {
  n <- nrow(X)
  nulldev <- fit$nulldev
  dev <- (1 - fit$dev.ratio[lambda_idx]) * nulldev
  k <- fit$df[lambda_idx] + 1  # include intercept
  aic <- dev + 2 * k
  aicc <- aic + (2 * k * (k + 1)) / (n - k - 1)
  return(list(aic = aic, aicc = aicc))
}

# LASSO CV
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
lasso_cv_lambda <- lasso_cv$lambda.min
lasso_cv_pred <- predict(lasso_cv, newx = X_test, s = lasso_cv_lambda)
lasso_cv_cor <- cor(lasso_cv_pred, y_test[, voxel])
lasso_cv_mse <- mean((lasso_cv_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
lasso_cv_coef <- predict(lasso_cv, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_cv_lambda)
lasso_cv_df <- sum(lasso_cv_coef[-1] != 0)  # number of non-zero coefficients excluding intercept

# LASSO AIC
lasso_full <- glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 1)
aic_vals <- sapply(1:length(lasso_full$lambda), function(i) {
  compute_aic(lasso_full, X_train, y_train[, voxel], i)$aic
})
idx_aic <- which.min(aic_vals)
lasso_aic_lambda <- lasso_full$lambda[idx_aic]
lasso_aic_pred <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_test, s = lasso_aic_lambda)
lasso_aic_cor <- cor(lasso_aic_pred, y_test[, voxel])
lasso_aic_mse <- mean((lasso_aic_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
lasso_aic_coef <- predict(lasso_full, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_aic_lambda)
lasso_aic_df <- sum(lasso_aic_coef[-1] != 0)

# LASSO AICc
aicc_vals <- sapply(1:length(lasso_full$lambda), function(i) {
  compute_aic(lasso_full, X_train, y_train[, voxel], i)$aicc
})
idx_aicc <- which.min(aicc_vals)
lasso_aicc_lambda <- lasso_full$lambda[idx_aicc]
lasso_aicc_pred <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_test, s = lasso_aicc_lambda)
lasso_aicc_cor <- cor(lasso_aicc_pred, y_test[, voxel])
lasso_aicc_mse <- mean((lasso_aicc_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
lasso_aicc_coef <- predict(lasso_full, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_aicc_lambda)
lasso_aicc_df <- sum(lasso_aicc_coef[-1] != 0)

# Ridge CV
ridge_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
ridge_cv_lambda <- ridge_cv$lambda.min
ridge_cv_pred <- predict(ridge_cv, newx = X_test, s = ridge_cv_lambda)
ridge_cv_cor <- cor(ridge_cv_pred, y_test[, voxel])
ridge_cv_mse <- mean((ridge_cv_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
ridge_cv_coef <- predict(ridge_cv, type = "coefficients", s = ridge_cv_lambda)
ridge_cv_df <- sum(ridge_cv_coef[-1] != 0)  # Ridge non-zero? all non-zero but we can count non-zero as df = p
ridge_cv_df <- ncol(X_train)  # Ridge uses all predictors

# Model comparison
df <- data.frame(
  Model = c("LASSO_CV", "LASSO_AIC", "LASSO_AICc", "Ridge_CV"),
  Correlation = c(lasso_cv_cor, lasso_aic_cor, lasso_aicc_cor, ridge_cv_cor),
  MSE = c(lasso_cv_mse, lasso_aic_mse, lasso_aicc_mse, ridge_cv_mse),
  Lambda = c(lasso_cv_lambda, lasso_aic_lambda, lasso_aicc_lambda, ridge_cv_lambda),
  Df = c(lasso_cv_df, lasso_aic_df, lasso_aicc_df, ridge_cv_df)
)
print(df)
write.csv(df, "/app/model_comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Determine best model based on highest correlation
best_idx <- which.max(df$Correlation)
best_model <- df$Model[best_idx]
cat("Best model:", best_model, "\n")

# Retrain best model on all training data (fit_feat, resp_dat)
X_all <- fit_feat
y_all <- resp_dat[, voxel]
if (best_model == "LASSO_CV") {
  best_fit <- cv.glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
  best_lambda <- best_fit$lambda.min
} else if (best_model == "LASSO_AIC") {
  full_fit <- glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1)
  aic_all <- sapply(1:length(full_fit$lambda), function(i) {
    compute_aic(full_fit, X_all, y_all, i)$aic
  })
  best_lambda <- full_fit$lambda[which.min(aic_all)]
  best_fit <- full_fit
} else if (best_model == "LASSO_AICc") {
  full_fit <- glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1)
  aicc_all <- sapply(1:length(full_fit$lambda), function(i) {
    compute_aic(full_fit, X_all, y_all, i)$aicc
  })
  best_lambda <- full_fit$lambda[which.min(aicc_all)]
  best_fit <- full_fit
} else { # Ridge_CV
  best_fit <- cv.glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
  best_lambda <- best_fit$lambda.min
}

# Predict on validation images
val_pred <- predict(best_fit, newx = val_feat, s = best_lambda)
# Clamp to [-100, 100]
val_pred <- pmin(pmax(val_pred, -100), 100)
cat("Predictions range:", range(val_pred), "\n")
cat("Number of predictions:", length(val_pred), "\n")

# Write predictions
write.table(val_pred, "/app/predv1.txt", row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE)
EOF
",
      "duration": 1.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}

> # split data
> n <- nrow(fit_feat)
> train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = floor(0.5 * n))
> remaining <- setdiff(1:n, train_idx)
> val_idx <- sample(remaining, size = floor(0.5 * length(remaining)))
> test_idx <- setdiff(remaining, val_idx)
> X_train <- fit_feat[train_idx, ]
> y_train <- resp_dat[train_idx, ]
> X_val <- fit_feat[val_idx, ]
> y_val <- resp_dat[val_idx, ]
> X_test <- fit_feat[test_idx, ]
> y_test <- resp_dat[test_idx, ]
> voxel <- 1
>
> # function to compute AIC and AICc from glmnet object
> compute_aic <- function(fit, X, y, lambda_idx) {
>   n <- nrow(X)
>   nulldev <- fit$nulldev
>   dev <- (1 - fit$dev.ratio[lambda_idx]) * nulldev
>   k <- fit$df[lambda_idx] + 1  # include intercept
>   aic <- dev + 2 * k
>   aicc <- aic + (2 * k * (k + 1)) / (n - k - 1)
>   return(list(aic = aic, aicc = aicc))
> }
>
> # LASSO CV
> lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
> lasso_cv_lambda <- lasso_cv$lambda.min
> lasso_cv_pred <- predict(lasso_cv, newx = X_test, s = lasso_cv_lambda)
> lasso_cv_cor <- cor(lasso_cv_pred, y_test[, voxel])
> lasso_cv_mse <- mean((lasso_cv_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
> lasso_cv_coef <- predict(lasso_cv, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_cv_lambda)
> lasso_cv_df <- sum(lasso_cv_coef[-1] != 0)  # number of non-zero coefficients excluding intercept
>
> # LASSO AIC
> lasso_full <- glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 1)
> aic_vals <- sapply(1:length(lasso_full$lambda), function(i) {
>   compute_aic(lasso_full, X_train, y_train[, voxel], i)$aic
> })
> idx_aic <- which.min(aic_vals)
> lasso_aic_lambda <- lasso_full$lambda[idx_aic]
> lasso_aic_pred <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_test, s = lasso_aic_lambda)
> lasso_aic_cor <- cor(lasso_aic_pred, y_test[, voxel])
> lasso_aic_mse <- mean((lasso_aic_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
> lasso_aic_coef <- predict(lasso_full, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_aic_lambda)
> lasso_aic_df <- sum(lasso_aic_coef[-1] != 0)
>
> # LASSO AICc
> aicc_vals <- sapply(1:length(lasso_full$lambda), function(i) {
>   compute_aic(lasso_full, X_train, y_train[, voxel], i)$aicc
> })
> idx_aicc <- which.min(aicc_vals)
> lasso_aicc_lambda <- lasso_full$lambda[idx_aicc]
> lasso_aicc_pred <- predict(lasso_full, newx = X_test, s = lasso_aicc_lambda)
> lasso_aicc_cor <- cor(lasso_aicc_pred, y_test[, voxel])
> lasso_aicc_mse <- mean((lasso_aicc_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
> lasso_aicc_coef <- predict(lasso_full, type = "coefficients", s = lasso_aicc_lambda)
> lasso_aicc_df <- sum(lasso_aicc_coef[-1] != 0)
>
> # Ridge CV
> ridge_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train[, voxel], alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
> ridge_cv_lambda <- ridge_cv$lambda.min
> ridge_cv_pred <- predict(ridge_cv, newx = X_test, s = ridge_cv_lambda)
> ridge_cv_cor <- cor(ridge_cv_pred, y_test[, voxel])
> ridge_cv_mse <- mean((ridge_cv_pred - y_test[, voxel])^2)
> ridge_cv_coef <- predict(ridge_cv, type = "coefficients", s = ridge_cv_lambda)
> ridge_cv_df <- sum(ridge_cv_coef[-1] != 0)  # Ridge non-zero? all non-zero but we can count non-zero as df = p
> ridge_cv_df <- ncol(X_train)  # Ridge uses all predictors
>
> # Model comparison
> df <- data.frame(
>   Model = c("LASSO_CV", "LASSO_AIC", "LASSO_AICc", "Ridge_CV"),
>   Correlation = c(lasso_cv_cor, lasso_aic_cor, lasso_aicc_cor, ridge_cv_cor),
>   MSE = c(lasso_cv_mse, lasso_aic_mse, lasso_aicc_mse, ridge_cv_mse),
>   Lambda = c(lasso_cv_lambda, lasso_aic_lambda, lasso_aicc_lambda, ridge_cv_lambda),
>   Df = c(lasso_cv_df, lasso_aic_df, lasso_aicc_df, ridge_cv_df)
> )
> print(df)
> write.csv(df, "/app/model_comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)
>
> # Determine best model based on highest correlation
> best_idx <- which.max(df$Correlation)
> best_model <- df$Model[best_idx]
> cat("Best model:", best_model, "\n")
>
> # Retrain best model on all training data (fit_feat, resp_dat)
> X_all <- fit_feat
> y_all <- resp_dat[, voxel]
> if (best_model == "LASSO_CV") {
>   best_fit <- cv.glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
>   best_lambda <- best_fit$lambda.min
> } else if (best_model == "LASSO_AIC") {
>   full_fit <- glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1)
>   aic_all <- sapply(1:length(full_fit$lambda), function(i) {
>     compute_aic(full_fit, X_all, y_all, i)$aic
>   })
>   best_lambda <- full_fit$lambda[which.min(aic_all)]
>   best_fit <- full_fit
> } else if (best_model == "LASSO_AICc") {
>   full_fit <- glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 1)
>   aicc_all <- sapply(1:length(full_fit$lambda), function(i) {
>     compute_aic(full_fit, X_all, y_all, i)$aicc
>   })
>   best_lambda <- full_fit$lambda[which.min(aicc_all)]
>   best_fit <- full_fit
> } else { # Ridge_CV
>   best_fit <- cv.glmnet(X_all, y_all, alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
>   best_lambda <- best_fit$lambda.min
> }
>
> # Predict on validation images
> val_pred <- predict(best_fit, newx = val_feat, s = best_lambda)
> # Clamp to [-100, 100]
> val_pred <- pmin(pmax(val_pred, -100), 100)
> cat("Predictions range:", range(val_pred), "\n")
> cat("Number of predictions:", length(val_pred), "\n")
>
> # Write predictions
> write.table(val_pred, "/app/predv1.txt", row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE)
